title: Spec-Kit / SDD artifact layer
summary: Repo-native Spec-Kit layer that turns planning intent into machine-readable spec artifacts and task refs.

constitution.summary: Spec artifacts must stay commander-owned, repo-native, and directly linked to planning/tasking.
principles: Keep the spec as the truth source for scope and acceptance.
Do not copy the commander planning skeleton into a parallel memory system.
Route spec references through packet and context bundles instead of freeform chat.
guardrails: Do not replace commander docs as the truth source.
Do not invent a second plans/memory/reports/debt stack.
Do not let worker execution bypass packet and phase contracts.

specification.summary: Define the minimum machine-readable contract for spec-driven planning and tasking.
requirements: Expose constitution, specification, planning, tasking, and implementation_state as structured sections.
Record acceptance, non_goals, invariants, and truth_sources explicitly.
Make spec refs attachable to objective/phase/goal packets.
interfaces: Commander phase and objective plans can carry spec_refs.
Commander dispatch can route spec refs into packet.json, worker_brief.md, and context_bundle.json.
Workers can inspect the spec artifact without searching the whole repo.
constraints: Spec refs must remain repo-native and path-addressable.
The artifact must stay schema-valid and machine-readable.
Spec references must not become a new parallel planning hierarchy.

planning.summary: Bridge the current objective/phase/goal queue to a spec-first artifact model.

milestones.title: Spec schema and kit script
milestones.goal: Create the repo-native spec contract and validation helpers.

milestones.title: Packet and phase wiring
milestones.goal: Carry spec_refs through phase goals into dispatch packets and routing bundles.

milestones.title: Status and summary visibility
milestones.goal: Surface spec refs in phase/objective summaries for commander inspection.

tasking.summary: Specify how a worker should use spec artifacts during a dispatched task.
dispatch_rules: Read packet.json first, then the routed spec refs, then worker_brief.md, then context_bundle.json.
Use spec refs to narrow the implementation scope before editing code.
Treat the spec as a contract, not as optional inspiration.
task_contracts: Each packet may carry one or more spec_refs.
Each goal may inherit or override spec_refs at the packet level.
The dispatch layer must keep spec refs visible to the worker and to the commander.
packet_requirements: Packet schema must accept spec_refs as structured references.
Dispatch must keep spec refs in the packet and context bundle.
Worker brief must show the spec refs explicitly.

implementation_state.summary: The spec artifact layer exists and is being wired into the planning and dispatch path.
evidence: Spec artifact schema exists in commander/transport/schemas.
Spec kit helper exists in commander/transport/scripts.
A sample spec artifact lives in commander/specs/task-5-7-spec-template.json.
blockers: Need the packet and phase schemas to carry spec_refs end to end.
Need summaries and routing bundles to expose the spec refs.
notes: Use this artifact as the concrete contract for the current implementation slice.

acceptance: Phase goal packets can carry spec_refs without breaking phase plan validation.
Dispatch propagates spec refs into worker brief and context bundle.
Objective and phase summaries expose the spec refs to commander inspection.
non_goals: Do not create a parallel plans/memory/reports/debt hierarchy.
Do not move commander truth sources out of commander docs.
Do not auto-edit live specs without commander approval.
invariants: Spec refs are commander-owned and repo-native.
Spec refs travel with task packets, not with chat memory.
Every spec artifact remains machine-readable and schema-valid.
truth_sources: commander/outer/指挥官LangGraph运行时项目化方案.md
notes: This artifact is the seed contract for the current spec-template slice.
